info.title: Binderr API
info.description: This is the API documentation for the Binderr application. All APIs are rate-limited to 100 requests per second. To get your API key, contact your sales representative.

get.description: Get a full list of supported countries and nationalities. These are the only values allowed to be used in Binderr API.

post.description:
This endpoint is used to obtain an OAuth access token. Returned access token will be valid for
the specified time, refresh token will be valid for
  
 - `authorization_code` - half a year
  
 - `client_credentials` - 12h

This endpoint is used to obtain a new OAuth access token using a refresh token.
            Response also contains new refresh token, with a new expiration time.

get.description: Returns the current user information.
Returns team information of currently logged in user.
Returns a list of all documents related to the specified entity.

path.description: Path to document in the filesystem. To nest it in deeper folders, use a slash (`/`) as a separator. For example, `nested/folder`.

typeId.description: Document type ID. Users can only choose non-system document types.

get.description: Returns a list of all available document types.
Returns the specified document.

delete.description: Removes the specified document from the system.

get.description: Returns thumbnail of the specified document.
This endpoint is used to search for matching entities.

post.description: This endpoint is used to create a new entity. Note that if same entity already exists, then that entity will be returned instead.

get.description: This endpoint is used to search for matching entities, which are converted to minimal info entity (where fields for both individual and legal entity are mapped to same fields).
This endpoint is used to get entity by external ID.
This endpoint is used to get all industries. Industries are only used for legal entities.
This endpoint is used to get legal entity types.
This endpoint is used to get all managing (serviced by) entities. Our app has renamed managed by to serviced by and in requests we still use `managedBy`.

get.summary: Get all provided services for entity type
get.description: This endpoint is used to get all provided services for given entity type.
This endpoint is used to get all relationship statuses.
This endpoint is used to get entity by ID.
This endpoint is used to get basic entity information by ID. Good when you want to get a simple overview of the entity, as name for individual and legal entity is mapped to one field.

post.description: This endpoint is used to add provided service for given entity.

delete.description: This endpoint is used to delete provided service for given entity. Entity Provided Service ID comes from Entity `providedServices[]id`

get.description: This endpoint is used to get identity documents for an entity.
This endpoint is used to get all involvements related to entity.

post.description:
This endpoint is used to add or update involvements related to entity.
Role can be anything, but you can get existing possible values for role holder using [Get possible roles for entity](#tag/Involvements/operation/getInvolvementRoles).

**NB!** Role `Shareholder` cannot be added, you must actually add a shareholder through [Add shareholder](#tag/Shareholders/operation/addShareholder) endpoint!

delete.description: This endpoint is used to remove involvement role. The role needs to match exactly. If no `removalDate` is specified, then current date is used.

get.description:
This endpoint is used with adding new involvement to entity. There's 2 entity IDs in the
request: forEntityId is the role holder and entityId is where you want to add the involvement.


This is informative and you may choose to enter any other text value as role.
This endpoint is used to get all notes for an entity.

post.description: This endpoint is used to add or update note on an entity. To add documents, first upload them using Documents -> Upload document and then add the IDs of document into the request.

delete.description: This endpoint is used to delete a note.

post.description: This endpoint is used to add shareholder to current entity. Either use `sharePercentage` or `ownedShares` (if `ownedShares` exist, then percentage is automatically calculated from that).

get.description: This endpoint is used to get shareholding structure for current entity

delete.description: This endpoint is used to remove entity.

post.description: This endpoint is used to update specific fields of an entity. The structure to update is the same as response, eg. to update name of legal entity, you need to send field 'legalEntity.name' with desired value.

get.description: This endpoint is used to search all available forms.
This endpoint is used to get all forms that have been sent out.

parameters.name: searchTerm
parameters.description: Searches by entity name or form name

post.description: This endpoint is used to submit form.

This endpoint is used to create and send form to a specific entity. If email is provided, the form will be sent to that email address. Else, link is generated and returned in the response, where user needs to be redirected to.

get.description: This endpoint is used to list all REST webhooks for all event types.

post.description: This endpoint is used to create a new REST webhook, which will be triggered when a specific event occurs.
This endpoint is used to test the data of a specific REST webhook. Usually returns the latest data for the specified event.

404.description: No records found for the specified event.

delete.description: This endpoint is used to remove a specific REST webhook.

get.description: This endpoint is used to get all risk reviews for the requested entity.

delete.description: Removes the specified risk review from the system.

get.description: This endpoint is used to get verification status for the requested entity.

post.description:
This endpoint is used to request identity document for the requested entity. `ANY` document type means that user can pick themselves which document they'd like to upload.

 If email is provided, the request will be sent to that email address. Otherwise, link is generated and returned in the response, where user needs to be redirected to.
This endpoint is used to run screening for the requested entity.
This endpoint is used to toggle ongoing monitoring for the requested entity.

createdById.description: ID of the team member who created the account

ownedShares.description: List of shares owned by shareholder

countryCode.x-size-message: Country Code needs to be in [ISO 3166-1 alpha-3] spec (3 characters)

externalId.description: External ID of the entity. Can be null, can only be set when creating a new entity

birthCountryCode.x-size-message: Country Code needs to be in [ISO 3166-1 alpha-3] spec (3 characters)

taxResidenceCountryCode.x-size-message: Country Code needs to be in [ISO 3166-1 alpha-3] spec (3 characters)

incorporationCountryCode.x-size-message: Country Code needs to be in [ISO 3166-1 alpha-3] spec (3 characters)

status.description: NB! This will become required in the future.

IdAndNameDto.description: NB! This will become required in the future.

visibleAt.description: Timestamp when the MARKETPLACE form link becomes visible to the managing company. Needed because the original creation timestamp precedes the bank's access.

filter.description: Additional filter criteria for triggering the webhook. Possible structure depends on event type.

updateIfEmpty.title: If enabled, the value is removed when field value is null or empty.

tags.name: Authentication
tags.description:
## OAuth authentication and authorization

To use OAuth, you need to have client ID and secret (which our support can provide). You also must provide information,
which type of authentication flow you want to use.


Currently, we support 2 ways of authentication:
- authenticating on behalf of a user, using `authorization_code`
- authenticating using client credentials, using `client_credentials`

### Authentication on behalf of a user
This is meant for user-facing applications and will give you user credentials. Your credentials (client ID and secret)
are not tied to any actual client (so you can authenticate against any user in any managing company).


**Flow:**
- Redirect user to our oauth page with `redirect_uri` pointing back to your page
- User logs in
- User is redirected to `redirect_uri` with `code` parameter
- You can use `code` to obtain access token from [access token request](#tag/Authentication/operation/obtainAccessToken) endpoint

> OAuth page:
> ```
> https://napkin.binderr.com/oauth2?client_id={YOUR_CLIENT_ID}&redirect_uri={YOUR_REDIRECT_URI}
> ```

### Authentication using client credentials
This is meant for server-to-server communication and will give credentials with all rights to the account. Your credentials (client ID and secret)
are tied to a specific client (so you can do actions only on behalf of that client). All actions done using these credentials will be logged as `API User`.


For this **flow**, you just need to send `client_id` and `client_secret` in the request body to 
[access token request](#tag/Authentication/operation/obtainAccessToken) endpoint.
